2605.07931.title: One Token Per Frame: Reconsidering Visual Bandwidth in World Models for VLA Policy
2605.07931.paper_summary: Vision-language-action (VLA) models increasingly rely on auxiliary world modules to plan over long horizons, yet how such modules should be parameterized on top of a pretrained VLA remains an open design question. Existing world-model-augmented VLAs typically pass the per-frame visual stream into the world module at high visual bandwidth and treat its rollout as a side product of action prediction; under a constrained adaptation budget on a frozen backbone, this leaves both the per-frame representation and the latent action coupling under-examined. We introduce OneWM-VLA, which compresses each view into a single semantic token per frame through an Adaptive Attention Pooling, and produces the resulting latent stream and the action trajectory under a single flow-matching objective rather than connecting them through a separate decoder. Empirically, we find that per-frame visual bandwidth can be reduced to a single token without compromising long-horizon performance under our setup. Trained with 14.71M LoRA parameters on a $π_0$ (2B) backbone, OneWM-VLA improves the average success rate from 47.9% to 61.3% on MetaWorld~MT50, reaches 95.6% on LIBERO-Long (vs.85.2% for $π_0$), and reaches 60.0% on the long-horizon deformable task Fold Cloth on a real Piper arm (vs.20.0% for $π_0$).
2605.07931.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言-行動 (VLA) 模型越來越依賴輔助世界模組來進行長期規劃，然而如何在預訓練的 VLA 上對這些模組進行參數化仍然是一個未解的設計問題。現有的世界模型增強 VLA 通常將每幀的視覺流以高視覺帶寬傳入世界模組，並將其展開視為行動預測的副產品；在對凍結主幹的受限適應預算下，這使得每幀的表示和潛在行動耦合都未受到充分檢視。我們提出了 OneWM-VLA，該模型通過自適應注意力池化將每個視圖壓縮為每幀一個單一語義標記，並在單一流匹配目標下生成結果潛在流和行動軌跡，而不是通過單獨的解碼器將它們連接起來。經驗上，我們發現每幀的視覺帶寬可以減少到單一標記，而不會在我們的設置下妥協長期性能。在 $π_0$ (2B) 主幹上訓練了 14.71M LoRA 參數的 OneWM-VLA，將 MetaWorld~MT50 的平均成功率從 47.9% 提高到 61.3%，在 LIBERO-Long 上達到 95.6%（相比 $π_0$ 的 85.2%），並在真實的 Piper 手臂上對長期變形任務 Fold Cloth 達到 60.0%（相比 $π_0$ 的 20.0%）。
2605.07931.author: Zuojin Tang et.al.
2605.07931.authors: Zuojin Tang, Shengchao Yuan, Xiaoxin Bai, Zhiyuan Jin, De Ma, Gang Pan, Bin Liu
